---
title: "Sample Description Lake Zurich"
output:
  html_document:
    code_folding: hide
---

<style type="text/css">
.main-container {
  max-width: 1800px;
  margin-left: auto;
  margin-right: auto;
}
</style>

Setup:

___
* Libraries:
```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#Libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(readxl)
library(cmocean)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(plotly)
library(openxlsx)
library(patchwork)
library(hrbrthemes)
library(webshot)
library(reticulate)
library(webexercises)
```
___
___
* Functions:
```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#Lake profile extrapolation function
LakeProfile <- function(dataset, variable, start_depth, end_depth, calc_interval, start_date, end_date) {
  
  # Description:
  
  #   This function generates data for lake profiles. It takes measurements as input and extrapolates in the given 
  #   interval values for the given time, depth, variable.
  
  # Inputs:
  
  #   dataset -> just give the name of the dataframe (type = dataframe)
  #   variable -> give the name of the variable of interest between ""(type = character) -> variable itself must be type integer/numerical
  #   stat_depth -> give the depth as an integer (type = int)
  #   end_depth -> give the depth as an integer (type = int)
  #   calc_interval -> give the extrapolation inverval as an integer (type = int)
  #   start_date -> give the date in the format "yyyy-mm-dd" (type = character)
  #   end_date -> give the date in the format "yyyy-mm-dd" (type = character)
  
  # Outputs: 
  
  #  Dataframe containing extrapolation for the chosen variable "variablename"
  
  innerfunction1 <- function(target_date, target_depth, dataset, variable) {
    data_for_date <- dataset %>% 
      filter(date == target_date) %>%
      arrange(depth)
    approx(data_for_date$depth, data_for_date[[variable]], xout = target_depth)$y
  }
  
  innerfunction2 <- function(target_depth, target_date, dataset, variable) {
    data_for_depth <- interp_depth %>% 
      filter(depth == target_depth) %>%
      arrange(date)
    data_for_depth$date <- as.Date(data_for_depth$date)
    approx(data_for_depth$date, data_for_depth[[variable]], xout = target_date)$y
  }
  
  interp_depth <- crossing(tibble(date = unique(dataset$date)), tibble(depth = seq(start_depth, end_depth, length.out = calc_interval))) %>% 
    group_by(date) %>% 
    mutate(!!variable := innerfunction1(date[1], depth, dataset, variable))
  
  raster <- crossing(tibble(date = seq(as.Date(start_date), as.Date(end_date), by = 1)), tibble(depth = unique(interp_depth$depth))) %>%
    group_by(depth) %>%
    mutate(!!variable := innerfunction2(depth[1], date, interp_depth, variable))
  
  return(raster)
}

#Function to Plot the Profiles
PlotProfiles_Flexible <- function(profile, variable, interval_legend_ticks, interval_contour_lines, plot_title, legend_title, color_palette) {
  
  # Description:
  
  #   This function generates lake profile plots using plotly
  
  # Inputs:
  
  #   profile -> dataset for the given lake profile (type = dataframe)
  #   variable -> variable for which the profile should be generated ""(type = character)
  #   interval_legend_ticks -> interval of the ticks in the legend (type = int)
  #   interval_legend_ticks -> at which interval should a contour line be drawn (type = int)
  #   plot_title -> give the title of the plot between "" (type = character)
  #   legend_title -> give the legend title between "" (type = character)
  #   color_palette -> give a desired color pallette from rcolorbrewer between "" (type = character)
  
  # Outputs: 
  
  #  Dataframe containing extrapolation for the chosen variable "variablename"
  
  #Set Plot Settings
  min_val <- min(profile[[variable]], na.rm = TRUE)
  max_val <- max(profile[[variable]], na.rm = TRUE)
  legend_ticks <- round(seq(min_val, max_val,by = interval_legend_ticks))
  
  #Plot
  plotly_plot <- plot_ly(x = profile$date,
        y = profile$depth,
        z = profile[[variable]],
        type = "contour",
        colors = color_palette,
        contours = list(
                 start = min_val,
                 end = max_val,
                 size = interval_contour_lines,
                 coloring = 'heatmap',
                 showlines = TRUE
               ),
        colorbar = list(
                 title = legend_title,
                 tickmode = "array",
                 tickvals = legend_ticks, 
                 ticktext = legend_ticks
               )) %>%
  layout(yaxis = list(autorange = "reversed", title = "Depth [m]")) %>%
  layout(xaxis = list(title = "Date")) %>%
  layout(title = list(text = plot_title)) %>%
  colorbar(title = legend_title)

  return(plotly_plot)
}

#Function to Plot the Profiles
PlotProfiles_Fixed <- function(profile, variable, plot_title, legend_title, color_palette) {
  
  # Description:
  
  #   This function generates lake profile plots using plotly
  
  # Inputs:
  
  #   profile -> dataset for the given lake profile (type = dataframe)
  #   variable -> variable for which the profile should be generated ""(type = character)
  #   plot_title -> give the title of the plot between "" (type = character)
  #   legend_title -> give the legend title between "" (type = character)
  #   color_palette -> give a desired color pallette from rcolorbrewer between "" (type = character)
  
  # Outputs: 
  
  #  Dataframe containing extrapolation for the chosen variable "variablename"
  
  #Plot
  plotly_plot <- plot_ly(x = profile$date,
        y = profile$depth,
        z = profile[[variable]],
        type = "contour",
        colors = color_palette) %>% 
  layout(yaxis = list(autorange = "reversed", title = "Depth [m]")) %>%
  layout(xaxis = list(title = "Date")) %>%
  layout(title = list(text = plot_title)) %>%
  colorbar(title = legend_title)

  return(plotly_plot)
}
```
___
___
* Data:
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#Load the data
abiotic_LT_dat <- read_excel("Data/Data_2009-2023.xlsx", sheet = "abiotic")
abiotic_ST_dat <- read_excel("Data/Data_2021.xlsx", sheet = "abiotic")
biotic_ST_dat <- read_excel("Data/Data_2021.xlsx", sheet = "biotic")
```
___

### Sampling site and map of lake zurich
```{r}
# ============================================================
# Nature-style overview map of Lake Zurich sampling sites
#
# FEATURES
# --------
# - Shows two study locations:
#   1) LZ-BITS sampling site:
#      47°19′15.2″ N, 8°33′32.0″ E
#   2) Long term monitoring sampling site:
#      47°18′16″ N, 8°34′31″ E
# - Downloads Lake Zurich polygon from OpenStreetMap
# - Includes Zurich for orientation
# - Adds a north arrow
# - Uses the confirmed working Overpass endpoint:
#     https://lz4.overpass-api.de/api/interpreter
# - Clean, publication-style map appearance
#
# OUTPUT
# ------
# Lake_Zurich_two_sampling_sites.png
# ============================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(sf)
  library(dplyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(osmdata)
  library(rnaturalearth)
  library(rnaturalearthdata)
  library(ggspatial)
})

# ============================================================
# 1) Sampling locations
# ============================================================
# LZ-BITS sampling site
sample1_lat <- 47 + 19/60 + 15.2/3600
sample1_lon <- 8 + 33/60 + 32.0/3600

# Long term monitoring sampling site
sample2_lat <- 47 + 18/60 + 16/3600
sample2_lon <- 8 + 34/60 + 31/3600

sites_df <- data.frame(
  name = c("LZ-BITS sampling site", "Long term monitoring sampling site"),
  lon  = c(sample1_lon, sample2_lon),
  lat  = c(sample1_lat, sample2_lat),
  coord_label = c(
    "47°19′15.2″N, 8°33′32.0″E",
    "47°18′16″N, 8°34′31″E"
  ),
  fill_col = c("#c62828", "#7b1fa2")  # red, violet
)

sampling_sites <- st_as_sf(
  sites_df,
  coords = c("lon", "lat"),
  crs = 4326
)

site_xy <- st_coordinates(sampling_sites)

sites_plot_df <- sites_df |>
  mutate(
    x = site_xy[, 1],
    y = site_xy[, 2]
  )

# ============================================================
# 2) Download Lake Zurich polygon from OSM
# ============================================================
set_overpass_url("https://lz4.overpass-api.de/api/interpreter")

q <- opq(
  bbox = c(8.35, 47.10, 8.95, 47.45)
) |>
  add_osm_feature(key = "natural", value = "water")

osm <- osmdata_sf(q)

water_polys <- bind_rows(
  osm$osm_polygons,
  osm$osm_multipolygons
)

lake <- water_polys |>
  filter(!is.na(name)) |>
  filter(name %in% c("Zürichsee", "Zurichsee", "Lake Zurich"))

# Fallback if name-based match fails
if (nrow(lake) == 0) {
  message("Named Lake Zurich polygon not found; using largest water polygon in query area.")
  water_proj <- st_transform(water_polys, 2056)
  lake <- water_polys[which.max(st_area(water_proj)), ]
}

lake <- st_make_valid(lake)

# ============================================================
# 3) Get Zurich for orientation
# ============================================================
places <- ne_download(
  scale = 10,
  type = "populated_places",
  category = "cultural",
  returnclass = "sf"
)

bb <- st_bbox(lake)

cities <- places |>
  filter(
    LONGITUDE >= bb["xmin"] - 0.05,
    LONGITUDE <= bb["xmax"] + 0.05,
    LATITUDE  >= bb["ymin"] - 0.05,
    LATITUDE  <= bb["ymax"] + 0.05
  ) |>
  filter(NAME %in% c("Zurich", "Zürich"))

city_coords <- st_coordinates(cities)

cities_df <- cities |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  mutate(
    x = city_coords[, 1],
    y = city_coords[, 2]
  )

# ============================================================
# 4) Plot extent
# ============================================================
xpad <- 0.045
ypad_bottom <- 0.030
ypad_top <- 0.050

xmin <- bb["xmin"] - xpad
xmax <- bb["xmax"] + xpad
ymin <- bb["ymin"] - ypad_bottom
ymax <- bb["ymax"] + ypad_top

# ============================================================
# 5) Build Nature-like plot
# ============================================================
p <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(
    data = lake,
    fill = "#d9ecf5",
    color = "#6a8fa6",
    linewidth = 0.35
  ) +

  # LZ-BITS site (red)
  geom_sf(
    data = sampling_sites |> filter(name == "LZ-BITS sampling site"),
    shape = 21,
    size = 3.4,
    fill = "#c62828",
    color = "black",
    stroke = 0.25
  ) +

  # Long-term monitoring site (violet)
  geom_sf(
    data = sampling_sites |> filter(name == "Long term monitoring sampling site"),
    shape = 21,
    size = 3.4,
    fill = "#7b1fa2",
    color = "black",
    stroke = 0.25
  ) +

  # LZ-BITS label and coordinates above point
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = sites_plot_df$x[sites_plot_df$name == "LZ-BITS sampling site"],
    y = sites_plot_df$y[sites_plot_df$name == "LZ-BITS sampling site"] + 0.013,
    label = "LZ-BITS sampling site",
    hjust = 0.5,
    size = 3.6,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = sites_plot_df$x[sites_plot_df$name == "LZ-BITS sampling site"],
    y = sites_plot_df$y[sites_plot_df$name == "LZ-BITS sampling site"] + 0.006,
    label = "47°19′15.2″N, 8°33′32.0″E",
    hjust = 0.5,
    size = 3.0
  ) +

  # Long-term monitoring label and coordinates below point
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = sites_plot_df$x[sites_plot_df$name == "Long term monitoring sampling site"],
    y = sites_plot_df$y[sites_plot_df$name == "Long term monitoring sampling site"] - 0.010,
    label = "Long term monitoring sampling site",
    hjust = 0.5,
    size = 3.6,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = sites_plot_df$x[sites_plot_df$name == "Long term monitoring sampling site"],
    y = sites_plot_df$y[sites_plot_df$name == "Long term monitoring sampling site"] - 0.017,
    label = "47°18′16″N, 8°34′31″E",
    hjust = 0.5,
    size = 3.0
  ) +

  geom_sf(
    data = cities,
    color = "black",
    size = 1.1
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = cities_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = NAME),
    nudge_y = 0.008,
    size = 3.0
  ) +
  annotation_north_arrow(
    location = "tr",
    which_north = "true",
    pad_x = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    pad_y = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering(
      text_size = 7,
      line_width = 0.7
    )
  ) +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(xmin, xmax),
    ylim = c(ymin, ymax),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_void(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.4),
    plot.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6)
  )

print(p)

# ============================================================
# 6) Export as PNG
# ============================================================
ggsave(
  filename = "Lake_Zurich_two_sampling_sites.png",
  plot = p,
  width = 14,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 600,
  bg = "white"
)
```


### 1. Long term data - lake profiles
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#Calculate the Profiles
temperature_LT_profile <- LakeProfile(abiotic_LT_dat, "temperature", 0, 135, 100, "2009-01-07", "2023-11-08")
oxygen_LT_profile <- LakeProfile(abiotic_LT_dat, "oxygen", 0, 135, 100, "2009-01-07", "2023-11-08")
phosphate_LT_profile <- LakeProfile(abiotic_LT_dat, "phosphate", 0, 135, 100, "2009-01-07", "2023-11-08")
```

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=15}
#Plot the Profiles
PlotProfiles_Flexible(temperature_LT_profile, "temperature", 5, 1, "Temperature Profile", "C", RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "YlOrRd"))
PlotProfiles_Flexible(oxygen_LT_profile, "oxygen", 5, 1, "Oxygen Profile", "mg/L", rev(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "RdYlBu")))
PlotProfiles_Flexible(phosphate_LT_profile, "phosphate", 50, 5,"Phosphate Profile", "μg/L", RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "YlGnBu"))
```

### Spring bloom 2021 - lake profiles
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=15}
#Calculate the Profiles
temperature_ST_profile <- LakeProfile(abiotic_ST_dat, "temperature", 0, 40, 1000, "2021-02-22", "2021-06-07")
oxygen_ST_profile <- LakeProfile(abiotic_ST_dat, "oxygen", 0, 40, 1000, "2021-02-22", "2021-06-07")
chlorophyll_ST_profile <- LakeProfile(abiotic_ST_dat, "chlorophyll", 0, 40, 1000, "2021-02-22", "2021-06-07")
```

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=15}
#Plot the Profiles
PlotProfiles_Fixed(temperature_ST_profile, "temperature", "Temperature Profile", "°C", "YlOrRd")
PlotProfiles_Fixed(oxygen_ST_profile, "oxygen", "Oxygen Profile", "mg/L", rev(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(10, "RdYlBu")))
PlotProfiles_Fixed(chlorophyll_ST_profile, "chlorophyll", "Chlorophyll Profile", "μg/L", "Greens")
```

### Spring bloom 2021 - microorganisms

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=15}
#Plot bacterial- vs. hnf-abundance
biotic_plot <- plot_ly(biotic_ST_dat, x = ~date) %>%
  add_lines(y = ~bacteria, line = list(color = "#4682B4", width = 1.5), name = "Bacteria [cells/mL]") %>%
  add_trace(y = ~hnf, line = list(color = "#A0522D", width = 1.5), name = "HNF [cells/mL]", yaxis = "y2", marker = list(color = "#A0522D")) %>%
  add_markers(x = ~date, y = ~bacteria, marker = list(color = "#4682B4"), name = "Bacteria [cells/mL]") %>%
  layout(title = "Spring bloom 2021 - Epilimnion [1-8m]", xaxis = list(title = "Date")) %>%
  layout(plot_bgcolor = "white") %>%
  layout(showlegend = FALSE) %>%
  layout(yaxis = list(title = "Bacteria [million cells/mL]", titlefont = list(color = "#4682B4"), tickfont = list(color = "#4682B4"), tickvals = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8) * 1e6, ticktext = c("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7", "8"), range = c(0, 8e6)),
         yaxis2 = list(title = "HNF [thousand cells/mL]", titlefont = list(color = "#A0522D"), tickfont = list(color = "#A0522D"), overlaying = "y", side = "right", tickvals = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8) * 1e3, ticktext = c("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7", "8"), range = c(0, 8e3))) %>%
  layout(margin = list(l = 60, r = 80, b = 50, t = 50))
biotic_plot
```


```{r}
############################################################
# ST_Bacteria_Plot
#
# PURPOSE
# -------
# Reproduce bacterial abundance as a Spring Bloom 2021
# short-term (ST) lake profile in the same overall style
# as the ST plots in Sample_Overview.Rmd:
#   - profile interpolation in depth and time
#   - Plotly contour plot
#   - reversed depth axis
#   - restricted ST time window only
#
# INPUT REQUIRED
# --------------
# FC_FULL.csv with at least these columns:
#   - date
#   - depth
#   - bac_abu
#
# OUTPUT
# ------
# Interactive Plotly object + optional HTML export
############################################################

########################
## 0) Setup
########################
data_dir <- "/SynologyDrive/PhD/Projects/SpringBloom2021/DataAnalysis_SpringBloom2021/Supplementary/LakeProfiles/"
out_dir  <- data_dir

ST_START <- as.Date("2021-02-22")
ST_END   <- as.Date("2021-06-07")

# Same ST depth focus as the Sample_Overview ST profiles
ST_DEPTH_MIN <- 0
ST_DEPTH_MAX <- 40

########################
## 1) Libraries
########################
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(lubridate)
  library(plotly)
  library(htmlwidgets)
  library(RColorBrewer)
})

########################
## 2) Lake profile function
########################
# This follows the same logic as in Sample_Overview.Rmd:
# 1) interpolate across depth for each date
# 2) interpolate across time for each depth
LakeProfile <- function(dataset, variable, start_depth, end_depth,
                        calc_interval, start_date, end_date) {

  innerfunction1 <- function(target_date, target_depth, dataset, variable) {
    data_for_date <- dataset %>%
      filter(date == target_date) %>%
      arrange(depth)

    # Need at least 2 measurements for interpolation
    if (nrow(data_for_date) < 2) {
      return(rep(NA_real_, length(target_depth)))
    }

    approx(
      x = data_for_date$depth,
      y = data_for_date[[variable]],
      xout = target_depth,
      rule = 2
    )$y
  }

  interp_depth <- tidyr::crossing(
    tibble(date = unique(dataset$date)),
    tibble(depth = seq(start_depth, end_depth, length.out = calc_interval))
  ) %>%
    group_by(date) %>%
    mutate(!!variable := innerfunction1(date[1], depth, dataset, variable)) %>%
    ungroup()

  innerfunction2 <- function(target_depth, target_date, dataset, variable) {
    data_for_depth <- interp_depth %>%
      filter(depth == target_depth) %>%
      arrange(date)

    data_for_depth$date <- as.Date(data_for_depth$date)

    good <- !is.na(data_for_depth[[variable]])
    if (sum(good) < 2) {
      return(rep(NA_real_, length(target_date)))
    }

    approx(
      x = as.numeric(data_for_depth$date[good]),
      y = data_for_depth[[variable]][good],
      xout = as.numeric(target_date),
      rule = 2
    )$y
  }

  raster <- tidyr::crossing(
    tibble(date = seq(as.Date(start_date), as.Date(end_date), by = 1)),
    tibble(depth = unique(interp_depth$depth))
  ) %>%
    group_by(depth) %>%
    mutate(!!variable := innerfunction2(depth[1], date, interp_depth, variable)) %>%
    ungroup()

  return(raster)
}

########################
## 3) Plot function in ST style
########################
# Same general plotting logic as Sample_Overview.Rmd,
# but adapted for bacterial abundance.
PlotProfiles_Fixed <- function(profile, variable, plot_title, legend_title, color_palette) {

  plot_ly(
    x = profile$date,
    y = profile$depth,
    z = profile[[variable]],
    type = "contour",
    colors = color_palette
  ) %>%
    layout(
      title = list(text = plot_title),
      xaxis = list(title = "Date"),
      yaxis = list(
        autorange = "reversed",
        title = "Depth [m]"
      ),
      plot_bgcolor = "white"
    ) %>%
    colorbar(title = legend_title)
}

########################
## 4) Load and clean FC data
########################
fc_data <- readr::read_csv(
  file.path(data_dir, "/LongTermLakeProfiles/Data/FC_FULL.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

fc_data_clean <- fc_data %>%
  mutate(
    date    = as.Date(date, format = "%m/%d/%Y"),
    depth   = as.numeric(depth),
    bac_abu = as.numeric(bac_abu)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(date),
    !is.na(depth),
    !is.na(bac_abu)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    date >= ST_START,
    date <= ST_END,
    depth >= ST_DEPTH_MIN,
    depth <= ST_DEPTH_MAX
  ) %>%
  arrange(date, depth)

########################
## 5) Build ST bacterial profile
########################
# Same interpolation density used for ST profiles in Sample_Overview
# (0-40 m, 1000 interpolated depth steps, daily interpolation in time)
bacteria_ST_profile <- LakeProfile(
  dataset       = fc_data_clean,
  variable      = "bac_abu",
  start_depth   = ST_DEPTH_MIN,
  end_depth     = ST_DEPTH_MAX,
  calc_interval = 1000,
  start_date    = ST_START,
  end_date      = ST_END
)

########################
## 6) Plot
########################
# Purple-pink palette chosen to stay visually close to your
# previous bacterial abundance plot, while keeping the ST contour style.
bac_palette_ST <- colorRampPalette(
  c("#f7fcf5", "#f2e5ff", "#d4b9da", "#c994c7",
    "#df65b0", "#e7298a", "#ce1256", "#980043", "#440154")
)(100)

ST_Bacteria_Plot <- PlotProfiles_Fixed(
  profile      = bacteria_ST_profile,
  variable     = "bac_abu",
  plot_title   = "Bacterial Abundance Profile",
  legend_title = "Bacteria [cells/mL]",
  color_palette = bac_palette_ST
)

ST_Bacteria_Plot

########################
## 7) Optional export
########################
htmlwidgets::saveWidget(
  ST_Bacteria_Plot,
  file = file.path(out_dir, "ST_Bacteria_Plot.html"),
  selfcontained = TRUE
)

########################
## 9) Export static image (recommended)
########################

# Make sure kaleido is available
# install.packages("reticulate")
# reticulate::py_install("kaleido")

export(ST_Bacteria_Plot,"ST_Bacteria_Plot.png")
```
